manifest.title: Page-like Content Example
manifest.description: A page-like dashboard: a single body region of block-wrapped content leaves — a heading, two markdown prose paragraphs, and a captioned image — that reads like an article. Two document-scope variables (productName, releaseVersion) are interpolated into the heading and the prose via ${var} to demonstrate that content text is carried verbatim with variables substituted. Run: lattice resolve examples/page-dashboard.json. Structured under the page-content grammar (root -> body region -> block -> content leaf).

config.source:
Welcome to **${productName}**. This release, version ${releaseVersion}, brings page-like content blocks so a dashboard can read like a real document: a heading, prose, and imagery composed from the same declarative grammar.

Everything you see below is a block-wrapped content leaf. The resolver carries the text verbatim and substitutes any variable reference from the in-scope variable environment before validation.

config.alt: Diagram of a page composed from heading, markdown, and image leaves
config.caption: Figure 1. A page assembled from block-wrapped content leaves.

config.source:
## How it fits together

Each leaf — heading, markdown, image — is a non-positional content type, so the grammar requires it to be wrapped in a `block`. The block applies per-block concerns (title, visibility, theme) to the single leaf it carries.

Because the same interpolation pass runs over every leaf, the product name **${productName}** and version **${releaseVersion}** appear identically wherever they are referenced.
